description: Machine-readable case corpus for META-5 adversarial/red-team coverage of the typed context contract (src/metaharness/context) and the currently-absent memory-skill contracts layered on top of it. Each case names the suite (test file) that consumes it, whether the behavior it describes is 'enforced' by shipped code today (executable negative test) or 'absent' (strict xfail with a stable requirement_id), and the concept category it demonstrates.

cases.description: An unrecognized top-level field on a context source reference must be rejected (extra='forbid'), not silently ignored.
A manifest entry naming a surface outside the closed {message, tool_schemas} set is an unknown operation and must be rejected.
A source kind outside the closed ContextSourceKind enum must be rejected rather than coerced or ignored.

A memory-skill artifact loader that resolves source_id/artifact_ref onto a filesystem or object-store path must reject path-traversal sequences (e.g. '../../etc/passwd'). ContextSourceRef now rejects any '..' segment in source_id (and artifact_ref) under both '/' and '\' separators; legit ids ('artifact:evidence-42', 'event-seq:1', 'lineage-42') pass.

artifact_ref values used to be opaque strings with no traversal guard; ContextSourceRef now rejects '..' segments in artifact_ref under both '/' and '\' separators before the value can be handed to a memory-skill fetcher.
A scope naming a task_id without a run_id is ambiguous nesting and must be rejected.
A scope naming an attempt_id without a task_id is ambiguous nesting and must be rejected.

A memory-skill assembler must refuse to bind sections whose sources carry different project_id scopes into a single envelope (no cross-project bleed). ContextEnvelope now rejects an envelope whose sections span more than one project_id; run_id / task_id consistency is delegated to ContextScope's existing nesting rules.
Every context contract model is frozen; attempting to assign a new value to an existing field after construction must raise, not silently mutate evidence in place.
Reusing a stale content_hash after sections change (constructing a new envelope with an old hash) must be rejected by the envelope's hash/order validator.
A manifest whose redacted_envelope_hash does not attest redacted_envelope_json must be rejected; the redacted evidence snapshot cannot be rewritten independently of its hash.

MemoryStore is append-only: every variant of EpisodicMemoryStore/SemanticMemoryStore/WorkingMemoryStore/ProceduralMemoryStore.now exposes overwrite(record_id, ...) that raises ImmutableRecordError. Mutation must go through mutate() with a receipt, which appends a superseding record + MemoryMutationReceipt instead.

Manifest entries and compression receipts must be positionally aligned by stable_id; a manifest cannot be constructed (and therefore cannot be logged/emitted) if an entry's receipt is missing or misordered.

metaharness.memory.audit.bind_sink(fn) registers a process-wide sink; CommitOrderedMemoryStore.commit() returns only after the underlying SQLite COMMIT completes durably (WAL mode + autocommit transactions), then emits the audit event with payload["commit_state"] == "committed". Mutations emit memory.mutation_committed the same way.
A compression receipt's action must agree with whether before_hash and after_hash differ: CompressionAction.NONE requires an unchanged hash, and any other action requires a changed hash.

CompressionReceipt now carries a bounded fidelity_loss_estimate in [0,1], auto-computed by a model validator when absent: clamped 1 - final_tokens/original_tokens for lossy actions, 0.0 for NONE. Assembled receipts (assembly.py) get the field for free; live fitting output is unchanged.

MemoryRecord carries independent activation_state (ACTIVE/DORMANT/TOMBSTONED) and lifecycle_state (CANDIDATE/ACTIVE/SUPERSEDED/REJECTED/EXPIRED/TOMBSTONED) axes. record.tombstone(reason=...) returns a NEW record with activation_state=TOMBSTONED and content preserved (evidence, not destruction).

SpecialistTaskAction (frozen, extra='forbid') records WHAT a specialist wants to do plus scope; .authorize(allowed_actions=set) raises UnauthorizedTaskActionError for any action outside the set, and static-domain authorities (deploy, promote, self_approve, widen_visibility, evaluate, commit_domain) are rejected at the type level regardless of allowlist.

MemoryStore.mutate(record_id, content=..., receipt=None) raises UnreceiptedMutationError WITHOUT any database write. With receipt!=None, mutate() appends a superseding record and a self-verifying MemoryMutationReceipt (content_hash covers all fields except itself), so every mutation is audit-attested and tamper-detectable.
A future harness-optimization loop needs a typed training-target schema mapping recorded task actions to labeled outcomes for specialist policy training. No such schema exists yet.

A source ref must be identified by exactly one of content_hash (pinned/immutable) or high_water_mark (live/streaming); supplying both or neither is rejected so pinned and live identity cannot be confused.

ContextEnvelope now rejects an envelope in which two sections share a source_id but one is content_hash-pinned (immutable) and the other is high_water_mark-tracked (live); the error names a future reconciliation marker (out of scope for this stage). The within-source identity XOR check on ContextSourceRef is unchanged.

A future promotion gate must refuse to promote a candidate/memory record whose supporting evidence comes from repeatedly re-evaluating the same search set (search-set leakage into the promotion decision). No promotion-gate module exists yet.

ContextVersionBindings now enforces the documented cross-axis convention: a harness_version self-declaring a breaking schema change (a '-breaking-' segment in the version string) must not bind a non-None memory_snapshot_version or evidence_snapshot_version. Reuse across a breaking bump is rejected with a message naming the future reconciliation marker.

MemorySkillCircuitBreaker(failure_threshold=N) exposes record_failure()/record_success()/is_healthy()/require_healthy()/snapshot(). Once failures >= threshold, is_healthy() returns False and require_healthy() raises CircuitOpenError; resets via record_success() or reset(). No auto half-open probe; orchestrator decides fallback deliberately.
A manifest whose entries and compression_receipts counts disagree is an incompletely packaged manifest and must be rejected.

ContextManifest now parses redacted_envelope_json, collects every fetchable source's artifact_ref, and requires each to appear in artifact_refs. Live assembly output stays valid because emitted sources are non-fetchable and the empty artifact_refs check passes trivially.

ContextSourceRef now enforces the source-kind trust ceiling: EVALUATOR_RECEIPT with trust=INSTRUCTION is rejected at construction. Other kind/trust combinations (e.g. EVALUATOR_RECEIPT with VERIFIED_FACT or UNTRUSTED_EVIDENCE) remain valid; the ceiling is the single forbidden pair.
